data.body:
PASS-1 review — criterion T3 (Integration feasibility) [overlay]: PASS / not-applicable. No findings.

Rationale (grounded):
- The T3 overlay applies only when a plan integrates an external API/CLI/service/library or asserts a previously-unused capability. This plan does neither.
- The Pass-4 coach explicitly REUSES an internal, already-used integration: "Reuses the rebar.llm single-turn structured-output path" / "the same cheap shape as the Pass-2 aggregate verifier." Verified in the codebase:
  - `src/rebar/llm/structured.py` — the single-turn structured-output reliability stack (output_mode, tolerant_parse, validate_to, bounded retry) is present.
  - `src/rebar/llm/runner.py` — the Runner seam exposes the generalized `structured` output `mode` + `output_schema` already (RunRequest), with a FakeRunner for offline exercise.
  - `src/rebar/llm/plan_review/passes.py` — Pass-2 is described as the existing single-turn aggregate verifier through the same Runner seam; Pass 4 is documented as the same shape.
  - `src/rebar/llm/aggregate.py` — the deterministic post-LLM clustering pattern the coach mirrors exists.
- Remaining dependencies (`ca03` finding-id minting, `db7b` sidecar, `9da1` shared layer) are internal cross-ticket contracts, not external integrations.
- No external CLI subcommands/API endpoints are named; no auth/HTTPS precondition is implicated; no asserted-but-unverified external capability is depended upon.

Per the T3 ANTI-FP clause ("an internal, already-used integration is not-applicable"), this is a clean chunk for T3.

Affirmations (T3): integration achievable as described (reuse of an existing internal path); no MISMATCH/UNVERIFIED external subcommand/endpoint risk; no missing auth/HTTPS precondition; no critical capability gap requiring a SPIKE.
